defs: The state of being drunken with, or as with, alcoholic liquor; intoxication; inebriety; -- used of the casual state or the habit.
quotes: The Lacedemonians trained up their children to hate drunkenness by bringing a drunken man into their company.

defs: Disorder of the faculties, resembling intoxication by liquors; inflammation; frenzy; rage.
quotes: Passion is the drunkenness of the mind.
synonyms: Inebriation. Drunkenness refers more to the habit
to specific acts. The first two words are extensively used in a figurative sense
a person is intoxicated with success
and is drunk with joy. “This plan of empire was not taken up in the first intoxication of unexpected success.”
